name: tpl-admissions-review
description: Admissions screening: pull application, score with AI, route to auto-admit/committee/reject, draft decision letter, and send

steps.prompt: Evaluate the admissions application for {{applicant_name}} applying to the {{program}} program for {{term}}. Application data: {{steps.pull-application.output}}. Produce a structured assessment with: (1) numeric_score — a score from 0 to 100 based on GPA (30%), essay quality (30%), extracurricular depth (20%), and program fit (20%); (2) score_breakdown — one sentence per dimension; (3) recommendation — one of 'auto_admit', 'committee_review', or 'reject'; (4) notes — 2-3 sentences on the strongest and weakest aspects of this application. Output as plain text with labeled sections.

Draft a formal admissions decision letter for {{applicant_name}} applying to the {{program}} program for {{term}} (application ID: {{application_id}}). AI recommendation: {{steps.score-applicant.output.recommendation}}. Committee decision (if applicable): {{steps.committee-review.output.committee_decision}}. Score breakdown: {{steps.score-applicant.output.score_breakdown}}. Write a professional, empathetic letter appropriate to the decision. For admissions: include congratulations, next steps, and enrollment deadline. For rejections: include an encouraging tone, brief non-specific reason, and information about reapplying. Length: 200-300 words.

steps.notifyMsg: Admissions decision processed for {{applicant_name}} ({{applicant_email}}), application {{application_id}} — {{program}} {{term}}. AI score: {{steps.score-applicant.output.numeric_score}}/100. Final decision: {{steps.score-applicant.output.recommendation}}. Committee override: {{steps.committee-review.output.committee_decision}}. Letter delivered: {{steps.send-decision-letter.output.delivered}}.
